title: Busy AI theme refresh (navy/green gradients), glowing CTAs, branded login/loading, and blank-UI fix

requirements.summary: Apply a lighter, readable navy/green gradient background across AppShell, Login, and AuthGate loading with strong contrast for text and surfaces.
acceptanceCriteria: All main pages (Chat, Maker, Analytics, Gallery, Shop) display a light navy/green gradient background without appearing blank.
Text on headings, body copy, and cards meets readable contrast against the new background (no near-invisible text).
The Login screen and the AuthGate loading screen use the same updated light navy/green gradient direction (no dark slate + blue/purple gradient).

file_operations.description: Replace global theme tokens to support a light navy/green aesthetic (background/foreground/card/border/muted) and add a reusable global gradient background utility (for AppShell/Login/AuthGate) that maintains readable contrast.

Switch the root app container from the current dark slate/blue gradient to the new shared light navy/green gradient background class and adjust sidebar/header surface classes so content remains clearly visible on the lighter gradient.

Update the Login page background to the same light navy/green gradient used elsewhere and revise card/surface styling so the layout remains readable (remove/avoid near-black overlays that can obscure content on a light gradient).
Update the initialization loading screen background to the same light navy/green gradient and ensure the loading content has high-contrast text/surfaces so it never appears blank.
Adjust page heading/text styles and card/surface classes away from purple-heavy and dark overlays to ensure readable contrast on the new light navy/green gradient.
Update maker landing cards and CTAs to use the new light theme surfaces and navy/green accents so feature cards remain readable and consistent on the new gradient background.
Update analytics page cards/inputs/surfaces to match the new light theme and ensure form text, borders, and chart containers remain clearly visible on the new gradient background.
Update Gallery page tab list/card empty state surface styling for the lighter navy/green background and ensure all text remains readable (no low-contrast muted text).
Update Shop page cards and accent colors to avoid dark/purple-heavy overlays and ensure strong contrast on the new light navy/green gradient background.

requirements.summary: Update app color tokens/gradients to de-emphasize purple and apply consistent navy/green styling across headings, cards, borders, and accents.
acceptanceCriteria: Primary/accent styling across pages no longer relies on purple-dominant gradients (e.g., blue→purple) and instead reflects navy/green styling.
Common UI elements (cards, borders, muted text, highlights) remain visually consistent across modules.

file_operations.description: Revise CSS variables for primary/accent/ring/border/muted to align with a navy/green palette (removing purple-dominant hues) and ensure consistent tokens used across cards, borders, and typography.
Update Tailwind theme extensions (e.g., glow shadow colors and any palette-related helpers) so built-in utility usage aligns with the new navy/green token system.
Replace purple-heavy gradients used in sidebar branding text with navy/green styling consistent with updated tokens; keep existing icon-forward navigation.
Update header surfaces and avatar fallback styling away from blue→purple gradients to navy/green, ensuring readable contrast and consistent accents.
Update active navigation button styling to use navy/green accent gradients and borders instead of blue→purple, while keeping existing icons and layout.
Adjust decorative butterfly tint from blue/purple-leaning styling to a subtle navy/green tone compatible with the new background (keep it minimal so it does not reduce readability).
Replace purple-forward heading gradient/text accents with navy/green equivalents and ensure muted text remains readable on the lighter background.
Update CTA gradients and any purple-forward accent usage (buttons, feature highlights) to navy/green equivalents while keeping existing icons and structure.
Update primary CTA gradient (Generate Analytics) and form input backgrounds/borders to align with new navy/green tokens and maintain consistent styling.

requirements.summary: Add animated/glowing styling for primary/key CTA buttons across the app with prefers-reduced-motion support.
acceptanceCriteria: Primary CTA buttons (e.g., Login button, “Get Started”, major submit actions) have a visible glow/animated emphasis on hover/focus.
When prefers-reduced-motion is enabled, button animations are reduced or disabled while maintaining a clear static hover/focus state.
Buttons remain readable and accessible (focus ring visible, no low-contrast text).

file_operations.description: Add global reusable CTA button utility classes (e.g., a navy/green gradient + glow effect) with hover/focus emphasis and a prefers-reduced-motion media query that reduces/disables animation while keeping a strong static hover/focus state.
Apply the new glowing/animated primary CTA styling to the Login button while keeping accessible focus styling and readable text.
Apply the new glowing/animated CTA styling to key 'Get Started' actions (primary flow buttons) and ensure focus rings remain visible.
Apply the new glowing/animated CTA styling to primary submit actions (e.g., Generate Analytics) while keeping reduced-motion behavior and accessible contrast.
Apply the new glowing CTA styling to the primary call-to-action button (e.g., Notify Me) while maintaining readability on the lighter theme.

requirements.summary: Increase logo/wordmark emphasis on the Login screen and AuthGate loading screen with prominent placement and consistent branding.
acceptanceCriteria: Login screen shows a prominent logo/wordmark at the top of the card (or above the card) that is clearly visible on the new light gradient.
AuthGate loading screen includes the same logo/wordmark (not only a spinner and “Loading...”).

file_operations.description: Add generated asset: frontend/public/assets/generated/busy-ai-logo.dim_512x512.png (Modern minimal monogram logo with navy + green palette on transparent background).
Add generated asset: frontend/public/assets/generated/busy-ai-wordmark.dim_1200x300.png (Horizontal wordmark/logo lockup in navy + green on transparent background).
Add generated asset: frontend/public/assets/generated/busy-ai-favicon.dim_64x64.png (Simple favicon mark derived from the monogram in navy + green).
Wire the generated favicon into the HTML head using frontend/public/assets/generated/busy-ai-favicon.dim_64x64.png.

Replace the current in-card 'BA' placeholder mark with a prominent logo/wordmark using frontend/public/assets/generated/busy-ai-wordmark.dim_1200x300.png (and/or the monogram) and ensure it is clearly visible on the new light navy/green gradient. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.

Add the Busy AI logo/wordmark to the identity initialization loading screen using frontend/public/assets/generated/busy-ai-wordmark.dim_1200x300.png (and/or frontend/public/assets/generated/busy-ai-logo.dim_512x512.png) and keep all user-facing text in English. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.

Update sidebar brand area to use the new wordmark (frontend/public/assets/generated/busy-ai-wordmark.dim_1200x300.png) for stronger logo emphasis without adding new decorative imagery. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.

requirements.summary: Preserve existing icons and avoid adding new large decorative images; only adjust imagery as needed for readability and consistency.
acceptanceCriteria: No new large decorative images are added to pages without explicit user placement instructions.
Existing icons remain in place; any icon/image changes are limited to fixes needed to support the new lighter theme and readability.

file_operations.description: Ensure branding updates use only the provided generated logo/wordmark assets (no additional large decorative images) and keep the UI icon-forward.
Maintain existing navigation icons and structure while updating only colors/surfaces for readability; do not introduce new decorative images.
Keep the existing lightweight decorative background behavior (no new imagery); only adjust tint/opacity if required to prevent readability issues on the new lighter gradient.

requirements.summary: Fix blank/near-invisible UI states by ensuring routes always render visible branded content during initialization and redirects, and by removing low-contrast overlays.
acceptanceCriteria: On first load: users see either (a) the Login page (if signed out) or (b) the app shell with visible page content (if signed in), without a blank screen state.
During identity initialization: the loading screen is visible and branded (logo + loading indicator) and does not render as near-black on near-black.
Cards/surfaces are adjusted so content does not blend into the background (no invisible borders/text due to low-contrast overlays).

file_operations.description: Eliminate the blank render when unauthenticated by rendering a visible branded 'redirecting/sign-in required' state (while navigating to /login) instead of returning null; also ensure initialization shows a branded loading view. Follow the authorization component's frontend guidance for authenticated vs guest UX (e.g., show a clear access state) and handle auth initialization gracefully. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.

Update ThemeProvider configuration to avoid defaulting the entire app into a dark theme that makes the new light gradient appear incorrect; ensure first load consistently renders visible content (routing + theming).

Audit and adjust global base styles and any overlay/animation utilities to prevent near-black-on-near-black or low-contrast text/surfaces; ensure default body/background/text tokens support the refreshed light gradient without blank-looking UI.
Adjust header surface transparency and borders so it stays visible and readable on the new light gradient (avoid overly dark translucent overlays that reduce contrast).
Update card/surface styling and heading accent colors to ensure content remains visible on the lighter theme (remove dark overlays that cause blending or invisible borders/text).
Update empty/loading states and tab container surfaces to maintain visible contrast on the new light gradient and avoid blank-looking panels.
